summary: Fixed two critical bugs in RX Expresss: 1) Public tracking page 'Order Not Found' error - fixed by configuring nginx to proxy /api requests to backend port 8001. 2) Driver portal not loading deliveries - fixed syntax error (extra closing brace) in Driver/Index.cshtml JavaScript. Both QR scan functionality and tracking page now working.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: Removed extra closing brace '}' on line 226 of Driver/Index.cshtml - was causing JavaScript syntax error preventing loadDeliveries() from being called
FIXED: Added nginx proxy configuration in /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/default to route /api/* requests to backend port 8001 and all other requests to frontend port 3000
seed_data_creation: Assigned order #5 (QR: M37DE3) to driver ID 1 for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both reported bugs are now fixed. The nginx proxy configuration is critical - without it, the frontend cannot reach the backend API. The extra closing brace in Driver/Index.cshtml was causing a silent JavaScript error that prevented the deliveries API from being called.

features_tested.Bug 1 - Public Tracking Page: FIXED - /Track/M37DE3 now displays order details with status timeline, delivery details, and recipient info
features_tested.Bug 2 - Driver QR Scan: FIXED - Manual QR entry works, modal closes on verify, success message shown, 'Scanned' badge appears
features_tested.Driver Login: PASS - Login with driver@test.com / Driver@123 redirects to /Driver/Index
features_tested.Driver Deliveries: PASS - Assigned delivery card displays with all details and action buttons

bug_1.description: Public tracking page showed 'Order Not Found' for valid orders
bug_1.root_cause: Nginx was not configured to proxy /api requests to backend port 8001 - all API calls returned 404
bug_1.fix: Updated /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/default to proxy /api/* to port 8001 and /* to port 3000

bug_2.description: Driver portal manual QR scan not working
bug_2.root_cause: JavaScript syntax error - extra closing brace '}' on line 226 of Driver/Index.cshtml prevented all JS from executing including loadDeliveries()
bug_2.fix: Removed the extra closing brace from startQrScanner() function
